mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS declines, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and business confidence, generating sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series deviates from its 12-month moving average by less than 1 standard deviation, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of observations when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%, or the signal loses predictive power outside the stable inflation regime.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms economic expansion and supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with payroll growth, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real GDP growth signals an imminent economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of quarters where GDP growth falls below the threshold, or the signal fails to beat a random walk benchmark in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and total employment indicate synchronized supply-side expansion and labor market strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regressions, or 20-day returns are negative in >55% of synchronized expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 2.5% increases the cost of capital and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days during periods of rising inflation.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of high-rate months, or the signal shows no predictive power when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% annually.
